definition: Next Token Prediction, also known as Language Modeling or Next Word Prediction, is a fundamental task in Natural Language Processing (NLP) where a model predicts the next token in a sequence of tokens given the context of the previous tokens. It is a crucial component of autoregressive models, which generate text one token at a time.
simple_explanation: Imagine you're trying to complete a sentence, and you've written a few words already - a next token prediction model would try to guess the next word that comes after what you've written, based on the context and patterns it has learned from a large dataset of text. For example, if you've written 'I love to', the model might predict 'eat' as the next token. This task is essential for applications like text generation, language translation, and chatbots.
technical_explanation: Next Token Prediction works by training a model on a large corpus of text data, where each token is predicted based on the context of the previous tokens, using mechanisms such as self-attention, recurrent neural networks (RNNs), or transformers. The model learns to capture patterns and relationships in the data, such as grammar, syntax, and semantics, and uses this knowledge to make predictions about the next token. Engineering trade-offs include balancing model complexity, training time, and evaluation metrics such as perplexity and accuracy.
investor_explanation: Robust configuration of Next Token Prediction directly affects system efficiency and business-critical accuracy metrics, driving cost savings and higher end-user retention.
examples: We optimized Next Token Prediction in our Natural Language Processing pipeline to improve performance.
Our team discussed the differences between Next Token Prediction and alternative solutions.
Understanding Next Token Prediction is highly beneficial when scaling our Language Modeling capabilities.
